card_description: Zebra is a divine or mythological figure referenced in Philip K. Dick's Exegesis. Appears in 83 Exegesis passages. Associated with dictionary terms: Zebra. Context: "Dick codifies his realization into a 26-point "Alphabet" of Gnosis, detailing the Fall, the role of Zebra (the restorer), the nature of the intercessor (unfallen primordial man), and the cosmic battle against the "Lord of Darkness."
